title: refactor(image): replace boolean reasoning with fast/balanced/pro modes
author: fisventurous

gist.headline: Thinking in three speeds
gist.blurb: We've upgraded our image generation reasoning from a simple on/off switch to three distinct gears: fast, balanced, and pro. Now you get precise control over quality and speed without worrying about the messy underlying model configs.
gist.summary: Replaced the boolean reasoning toggle for image generation with a new mode-based system (fast, balanced, pro) that correctly maps to model-specific configurations and fixes budget capping issues.
gist.impact: Developers can now fine-tune the balance between generation speed and reasoning depth, while backward compatibility ensures existing integrations keep working seamlessly.
gist.visual_concept: The pollinations bee mascot adjusting a three-speed dial or gear system on a retro computer monitor. The monitor shows a glowing brain or gears. The robot companion is watching excitedly.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of a retro computer terminal with a chunky three-speed dial. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender pastel gradient background. The main character is a cute pixel art bee mascot with a yellow body and dark brown horizontal stripes, small black legs, two black antennae, black vertical oval eyes, bright pink rectangular blush marks on cheeks, a small happy smile, and small rounded light blue translucent wings. She wears tiny engineer goggles and adjusts the dial, while a chunky pixel robot with a monitor head watches excitedly. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
